title: Personal Pronouns + Verb To Be
description: I / you / he / she / it / we / they — with am / is / are. The most important verb in English.
summary_pt_br: O verbo TO BE é a base do inglês. I AM, YOU ARE, HE/SHE/IT IS, WE/THEY ARE. Ele também existe onde o português usa 'estar', 'ter' (idade) e 'ser'.

exercises.explanation: Only one pronoun uses 'am' (I). Three use 'is' (he/she/it). Three use 'are' (you/we/they).
exercises.hint_pt_br: Memorize: só 'I' usa 'am'. Só 'he', 'she', 'it' usam 'is'. Todos os outros usam 'are'.

exercises.question: Complete: 'She ___ my sister.'
exercises.explanation: SHE → IS. Remember: he/she/it all use 'is'.

exercises.question: Complete: 'We ___ from Brazil.'
exercises.explanation: WE → ARE. Same as you and they.

exercises.question: Which is the contraction of 'I am'?
exercises.explanation: 'I am' → 'I'm'. Natives almost always contract. 'Im' without the apostrophe is a spelling mistake.
exercises.hint_pt_br: I am = I'm. A forma contraída é muito mais natural na fala.

exercises.question: In English, how do you say 'I'm hungry' (fome)?
exercises.explanation: English uses BE + adjective: 'I am hungry/tired/cold/thirsty'. Not 'I have hunger' (Portuguese structure).
exercises.hint_pt_br: Em inglês usamos 'to be' + adjetivo, não 'to have' + substantivo: I AM hungry (não 'I have hunger').

exercises.question: Question form: '___ you a student?'
exercises.explanation: Yes/no questions with BE start with the verb: Are you…? Is she…? Am I…?
exercises.hint_pt_br: Para fazer pergunta com 'to be', inverta: 'You are' → 'Are you?'

exercises.question: Which negative is correct?
exercises.explanation: Negatives with BE just add 'not' (or contract to n't): is + not = isn't, are + not = aren't, am + not = 'm not.
exercises.hint_pt_br: Negativa do 'to be': is/are + not. 'Isn't' e 'aren't' são as contrações.

exercises.question: Complete: 'It ___ cold today.' (right now)
exercises.explanation: IT is the pronoun for weather, time, and unnamed things. 'It is cold' / 'It's cold'.
exercises.hint_pt_br: Em inglês, 'it' é o pronome para tempo, clima e objetos genéricos.

scenes.title: To be drill
scenes.pt_summary: I am, you are, he is, she is, we are, they are.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I am Leo. I am a student.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, he is from São Paulo.

scenes.title: Listening story — Personal Pronouns + Verb To Be
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Zara and a student. I / you / he / she / it / we / they — with am / is / are. The most important verb in English. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: I am Leo. I am a student.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: Yes, he is from São Paulo.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Zara. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.
